title: rubus laxiusculus
intro: Rubus laxiusculus is a type of wild raspberry plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its lax, or loose, stem and greenish-yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rubus laxiusculus are said to include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with some herbalists also using it in topical creams and ointments for its potential skin-soothing and wound-healing properties.
